propositions: Add a `rebar audit serve` subcommand that starts a read-only FastAPI server bound to loopback serving an index of tickets with audit data.
Gate the web surface behind a default-false `[ui] enabled` config flag with full UiConfig wiring so it round-trips via toml, REBAR_UI_ENABLED, and -c overrides without an unknown-key warning.
Package web dependencies under a new `nava-rebar[ui]` optional extra and keep all fastapi/jinja2 imports lazy so `import rebar` stays inert when disabled or absent.
When disabled, `rebar audit serve` must refuse to start, name the `[ui] enabled` flag, and exit non-zero without importing web deps.
When the ui extra is missing, the lazy server import must yield an actionable install-nava-rebar[ui] message instead of a traceback.
Binding a non-loopback `--host` prints an exposure warning to stderr while the default 127.0.0.1 does not; the index page returns HTTP 200 listing a seeded audited ticket.
